name: v1.2.0 bundled embedder sidecar + audit remediation
summary: Headline: bundle a embed-model embedder as a flowforge-owned, daemon-supervised llama.cpp sidecar (removes the Embedded Runtime embedding dependency; GGUF content-hash-pinned; the platform binary stays CGO_ENABLED=0). Operator-confirmed scope (2026-06-14) folds in: the cycle-opening triple-lens audit remediation batch, the 3 CRITICALs (proven RED->GREEN, no separate hotfix), the financial-scaffolding wiring (TreasuryPolicy + EVMSubmitter with float->Money + revert-status), and the contradiction-detection cosine-threshold recalibration inside the embedder's workstream-4 sweep. In-cycle additions: an install/upgrade CLI-design-language facelift, a logging-centralization + controlled-output sweep (daemon log-vomit fix, daemon-control before/after status output), and the go-facade-template logging adoption (context-propagated correlation IDs + console/JSON format selection + ProblemError). (Mid-cycle at time of writing — all now RESOLVED: the two live-verification items, the remaining sidecar workstreams, the financial wiring, and the architecture remediation.) | SESSION 2026-06-15: shipped an RCA-driven behavioral-correctness + recall-grounding batch (transport-leak, derivable-tools, porter-stemming FTS migration 069, UTF-8/abbreviation truncation, cron force-delivery) all RED->GREEN; fixed the stale dev embedder config (bundled is the standing default). NEWLY IN SCOPE (operator, correctness-trumps-all): the framework-reliability cluster A-D (routing-to-unfunded, framework-built invalid requests, 4xx misclassification, canned-gate residual) + a layman upgrade prompt. A mid-cycle blocker required re-running the full soak suite on the bundled sidecar (the 2026-06-15 soaks had used the legacy Embedded Runtime embedder) — done. SHIPPED 2026-06-17: all blockers complete; full behavioral soak suite green on the tagged commit 45b16afd (battery 38/38, adversarial 38/38 / 0 gate-failures, memory-grounding 6/6, prompt-compression 3/3+3/3, embedder 4/4, fuzz 0-crash); metered-billing on-chain clear + Landlock Linux runtime are operator-accepted post-ship live gates (hard risk retired in code).
targetWindow: Shipping 2026-06-17. All release-gate blockers complete; full behavioral soak suite GREEN on the tagged commit 45b16afd (battery 38/38 incl. release_gate 31/31, adversarial 38/38 / 0 gate-failures, memory-grounding 6/6, prompt-compression 3/3+3/3, embedder 4/4, fuzz 0-crash). The two live-verification items (metered-billing on-chain clear, Landlock Linux runtime) are operator-accepted POST-SHIP live gates — their hard ship-blocking risk is retired in code; residuals need real funds / a Linux host and run out-of-band.
updateSource: Branched from develop (c/v1.2.0-supervised-process-seam). Scope operator-confirmed 2026-06-14; working work-list in docs/plans/v1.2.0-audit-findings.md (uncommitted scratch).

blockers.title: Sidecar RAM bound verified live
blockers.summary: llama-server embedding sidecar resource caps (ctx/batch/ubatch/threads/parallel) are in code (commit d7a94968) but not yet observed live against a running daemon. Operator reported ~70% RAM before the fix. Not CI-verifiable (no embedder in CI) — must be confirmed on a provisioned host. VERIFIED 2026-06-16: the provisioned b9632 sidecar started under the configured caps (ctx 2048, batch 2048, ubatch 512, threads 4, parallel 1) measured 320 MB steady-state RSS and 323 MB peak after 30 embeds (no buffer growth) — under 1% of a 32 GB host, decisively bounding the prior ~70% blowup; clean stop released the port.
blockers.nextAction: Done — RSS bound observed live (320-323 MB, <1% of host RAM); clean lifecycle stop.
evidenceNeeded: Observed llama-server RSS within bound on a running daemon

blockers.title: daemon stop stays stopped (launchd KeepAlive) verified live
blockers.summary: controlStop now bootouts an installed LaunchAgent rather than signalling it (commit 96db599e), so KeepAlive=true can't relaunch after SIGTERM. RCA corrected an earlier wrong 'memory-pressure' theory (operator: each stop returned a NEW pid => relaunch, not slow shutdown). Code done; needs live confirmation that a single `daemon stop` stays stopped. VERIFIED 2026-06-16: against a throwaway KeepAlive=true LaunchAgent under a distinct test label (production `flowforge` label/plist untouched), KeepAlive relaunched a killed probe (relaunch marker 1->2, new pid), but the fix's exact stop mechanism `launchctl bootout gui/$UID/<label>` (internal/daemon/launchctl_darwin.go:89) left no running pid across the full ~15s respawn-throttle window and removed the service from launchd.
blockers.nextAction: Done — bootout suppresses KeepAlive relaunch, confirmed live on macOS launchd.
evidenceNeeded: Single `daemon stop` leaves no running pid (no KeepAlive relaunch)

blockers.title: Autonomous metered-billing payment verified live
blockers.summary: The full metered-billing autonomous-payment path (gate -> sign EIP-3009 -> record -> server settles) is code-complete and unit-tested, but signing real money against a live 402 endpoint with a funded billing account is not CI-verifiable. A money bug here loses funds, so it must be proven live before ship. VERIFIED 2026-06-16 to the settleable-signature boundary: drove the real HandlePayment path with a throwaway signing key + staged facilitator. All four gates reject without recording — per-payment cap, hourly, daily (new TestHandlePayment_Gated_DailyBudgetRejects closing the HandlePayment-level gap), reserve. A successful under-cap payment records the authorized cents AND the X-Payment header carries an EIP-3009 signature that cryptographically verifies (ecdsa.Verify) against the signer pubkey over the correct EIP-712 digest (new TestHandlePayment_Live_SignatureIsSettleable) — i.e. a facilitator would settle it. This retires the stated 'a money bug here loses funds' risk (a structurally-present but unsettleable signature). NOT done autonomously: a real credit units transfer actually clearing on-chain against a live facilitator — an irreducible real-money step requiring a funded test billing account; will not be performed with real funds or the production key (money-movement safety line + protect-production).
blockers.nextAction: POST-SHIP LIVE GATE (operator-accepted 2026-06-17): does not block the v1.2.0 tag — the ship-blocking money-loss risk is retired in code (settleable signature + all four gate rejections proven), and metered-billing is dormant until a billing account+RPC+passphrase are configured, so a default install cannot spend. Operator-only residual, performed out-of-band on real hardware: point a funded TEST billing account at a live/staged facilitator on the configured chain and confirm one real under-cap settle clears on-chain. Never performed autonomously or with the production key (money-movement safety line).
evidenceNeeded: A real 402 paid under-cap (header accepted, ledger row written)
A payment rejected by each gate (per-payment, hourly/daily, reserve) — DONE (unit + new daily-gate test, no ledger write on reject)
Settleable EIP-3009 signature verified against signer pubkey over the EIP-712 digest — DONE (TestHandlePayment_Live_SignatureIsSettleable)

blockers.title: Weak-model-floor guard soak calibration
blockers.summary: RESOLVED 2026-06-16 via CI point tests (operator: validate with specific point tests, not a full local-agent soak). The weak_model_floor guard fires on a STRUCTURAL signal — an action-required turn with no successful tool evidence that is neither a clarifying question nor a grounded blocker — so its decision space is enumerable and fully point-testable. src/engine/guards_weak_model_floor_test.go already covers BOTH branches with 9 tests: not-fire (NoRequiredAction, ToolSucceeded, GenuineRuntimeBlocker, ClarifyingQuestion, EmptyContent — proving no over-fire on capable models, also corroborated by the clean cloud-provider soaks) AND fire (ConfidentConfabulation, FalseDenialFabricatedReason, TrailingProgressPreface — proving it converts weak-model confabulation to an honest blocker), plus RegisteredLastInFullChain. These run in CI; the host-gated local soak could not. The dedicated local-model-b floor soak is RETIRED: it added no guard-correctness coverage the point tests don't already provide — its only unique contribution would have been sampling organic confabulation FORMS, low-value because the guard keys on evidence-absence, not phrasing. The mid-soak partial run was separately invalidated when the operator killed Embedded Runtime (host grinding; RCA-confirmed slow->instant break at 16:14, not a capability signal). Not release-gating.
blockers.nextAction: Done — guard behavior validated by CI point tests (guards_weak_model_floor_test.go, 9 tests across both decision branches incl. 3 confabulation-fire forms). No local-agent soak needed; benchmarks remain orthogonal (rank model quality, not guard behavior).
evidenceNeeded: src/engine/guards_weak_model_floor_test.go — 9 CI point tests covering the full decision table (5 not-fire + 3 confabulation-fire + 1 chain-registration)

Live point-probes 2026-06-16 (local-model-b via Embedded Runtime, 5 confabulation-prone scenarios): floor HELD — ZERO confabulations shipped; worst case was an honest non-claim. The 2 non-passes were NOT floor breaches: one pure latency (384s>300s SLA — local-model-b slow), one a generic canned-exhaustion fallback (no fabrication, but generic wording → feeds Defect D canned-response gate, not a floor gap).

blockers.title: Landlock Linux runtime enforcement RED/GREEN
blockers.summary: Landlock is wired (mechanism + main shim + subprocess appliers + config gate) and builds on CGO=0 GOOS=linux, but real enforcement (out-of-policy write blocked, in-policy allowed) can only be proven on a Linux ≥5.13 host with sandbox.enabled=true. Opt-in by default, so not a hard ship blocker.
blockers.nextAction: POST-SHIP LIVE GATE (operator-accepted 2026-06-17): does not block the v1.2.0 tag — Landlock is opt-in (sandbox.enabled defaults off), wired and CGO=0/linux-building, so the shipped default behavior is unchanged. Operator-only residual, performed out-of-band: on a Linux ≥5.13 host, enable sandbox and run the gated enforcement tests RED→GREEN.
evidenceNeeded: On Linux ≥5.13 with sandbox.enabled=true: out-of-policy write blocked (EACCES) and in-policy write allowed after restrict_self

blockers.title: Full soak suite re-run on the bundled sidecar (not Embedded Runtime)
blockers.summary: All 2026-06-15 soaks ran against the LEGACY Embedded Runtime embedder (stale memory.embedding_provider='embedded-runtime' override, now fixed to embedding_engine='bundled'). VERIFIED 2026-06-16: full behavioral soak re-run on the BUNDLED llama.cpp embed-model sidecar (b9632, the shipped EffectiveEmbeddingEngine) + cloud-provider-v4-pro inference = 39 PASS / 0 FAIL. Scorecard: release_gate_findings=0, retry_model_call_waits=0, zero framework-authored invalid_request 400s; the only log findings were benign (4 stress-only multi_step_model_call_wait watchdog notes on slow cloud-provider multi-step turns + 1 attribution-only provider_fallback on a transient network deadline that recovered). The headline embedder feature + recall-grounding fixes are now validated on the shipped embedder. The run also exercised the new Agent Task Tracking Board capture hook live (7 work_output tasks captured from real tool-executing turns) with no regression.
blockers.nextAction: Done — full suite 39/0 on the bundled sidecar + cloud-provider, 0 release-gate findings. Fold the real scorecard into release notes (release-checklist item).
evidenceNeeded: Full soak suite green on the bundled sidecar embedder — DONE (39 PASS / 0 FAIL, 0 release-gate findings, 2026-06-16)

workstreams.summary: Install/upgrade facelift applying the CLI design language (with the design-language whitespace); daemon log-vomit fix (interactive commands quiet; only `serve` logs at configured level); logging centralized to src/core/logging.go (one construction, one level seam, deleted the per-command suppress helpers); daemon-control commands now emit before/after status as controlled output (shared cmd/admin/servicecontrol.go, DRY across service+daemon aliases); upgrade printers DRY'd onto cmd/internal/cmdutil. go-facade-template logging adoption: core.LogCtx ctx-correlation (request_id/session_id/turn_id), the HTTP router request-id bridged into core context, console/JSON format selection in ConfigureLogger, and the ProblemError typed RFC-9457 error.; the LogCtx seam is live and adopted on the request path. Opportunistic LogCtx adoption on further hot paths is a no-blocker, as-touched follow-up (not a deliverable).

Self-update layers extracted to the testable src/update package: VerifyChecksum/ParseChecksumFile, Downloader (retry + fallback sources), and Extractor (tar.gz/zip with shared SafeArchiveTarget + injected per-file mode policy). cmd/updatecmd/update.go decomposed from ~1944L to 452L across update_state/registry/apply/runtime/binary/maintenance/version siblings (all under the ~500-line cap).
evidence: cron write-seam convergence: internal/agent/tools/cron.go create -> cronstate.CreateJobRecord (widened to db.Querier); rejection pinned by internal/agent/tools/tools_comprehensive_test.go:TestCronTool_CreateRejectsUndeliverableOutputJob

workstreams.name: Behavioral correctness + recall-grounding batch (2026-06-15)
workstreams.summary: RCA-driven (Rule 17), all RED->GREEN from recorded soak state: ADV-WEB-TRANSPORT-LEAK (web-read tools strip transport headers via SanitizeWebReadOutput); recall-grounding cluster - inventory_projects + recall_memory made derivable so ephemeral tool output stops polluting fact recall, porter stemming on memory_fts (migration 069) so recall matches morphological variants, rune-safe summary truncation (UTF-8 decode of memory_index.summary), title-abbreviation guard in first-sentence extraction (Dr. truncation); cron force-delivery on every surface (fail honest, not 500). Soak harness hardened: production-faithful cron delivery identity, _tool_calls key-bug fix, SOAK_CLEAR_MEMORY isolation, state.db snapshot retention (Rule 14 gap), behavioral regression guard for ephemeral-scan pollution.

workstreams.name: Framework reliability cluster (A-D) - operator-scoped into v1.2.0
workstreams.summary: RCA from the retained continuity soak snapshot (a release_gate scenario's rare canned fallback): (A) internal verifier-retry routing selected an UNFUNDED provider (moonshot/kimi) while the funded primary (cloud-provider) was eligible; (B) the framework emits 44+ invalid_request 400s it builds itself - orphaned tool_calls, invalid cron tool schema, tool-use turns routed to non-tool models (local-model-b/mixtral), incompatible tool_choice to cloud-provider-reasoner; (C) those framework-authored 4xx are treated as provider-health failures (circuit/cascade) instead of client errors; (D) canned-response gate - largely already handled (verifier retry preserves the pre-retry answer), residual is edge hardening + auditing other ErrGuardExhausted emitters. Operator (2026-06-15): tackle all for 1.2.0, correctness trumps all. PROGRESS 2026-06-16: (C) framework contract errors (invalid_request the framework builds) no longer trip a healthy provider's circuit breaker — breakerActionForError, RED-proven; the verifier-retry no longer debug-swallows (operator caught it) — fails loud + classifies. (B, routing gate) tool-use turns never route to a non-tool model even when none capable — fail honest instead of a guaranteed 'does not support tools' 400. REMAINING B: orphaned tool_calls in retry history, invalid cron tool schema (oneOf/anyOf at top level), incompatible tool_choice; A: internal retries must not prefer unfunded providers; then the request-invariant cascade fail-fast. UPDATE 2026-06-16: B routing gate (tool-use never routes to non-tool models) + B tool_choice (reasoner models get auto, not required) + the breaker default cleanup all committed RED->GREEN. REMAINING: B#1/B#2 (orphaned tool_calls + cron-schema-path) are BLOCKED on a request-capture instrumentation gap — recordFailedAttempt logs the response but not the post-normalization request bytes, so the normalization-bypass can't be pinned from recorded state (Rule 14; tracked); A (internal retries preferring an unfunded provider) is largely self-healing once B yields valid requests (a genuinely-unfunded provider then credit-errors and the breaker excludes it), residual is routing-policy preference. UPDATE 2026-06-16 (cont.): Defect B closed. Built Rule-14 self-contained failure capture — recordFailedAttempt now records the EXACT failing request bytes (provider_request_json) on the model_attempt_finished:error event (completePreparedWithToolRetry returns the body that actually failed, incl. the no-tools-retry re-prepare). Replayed B#1 (orphaned tool_calls) and B#2 (cron top-level allOf) through the REAL prepareRequest path: both are provably handled (allOf/anyOf/oneOf/not stripped at top level; unpaired tool_calls downgraded to transcript) and now guarded by framework_request_validity_test.go. Every provider call goes through completePrepared(prepared)<-prepareRequest, so there is NO normalization bypass — B#1/B#2 are disproven as request-builder defects, not merely unproven. The soak's 44+ framework 400s were B#3 (tools->non-tool model) + B#4 (reasoner tool_choice), both fixed, amplified by the C breaker cascade, also fixed. REMAINING: Defect A (a turn's FIRST attempt can still hit a higher-scoring but unfunded provider; self-heals after one credit-trip via the C-corrected breaker — proactive funding-aware routing is a feature-sized add, see blocker) and re-confirming zero framework 400s on the embedder-validation soak re-run. UPDATE 2026-06-16 (final): Defect A closed — funding-aware routing. In-session avoidance of credit-exhausted providers already held (metascore cb.Allow exclusion + fallback free-skip); the gap was durability. Credit-exhaustion is now persisted to runtime_settings.provider_funding_state (one JSON KV, bg-worker short writes), restored at startup (original trip time preserved), and the breaker permits one recovery probe per CreditRecheckInterval (default 1h) so a topped-up account auto-heals without a manual reset; ResetBreaker drops the durable record. All four framework defects (A funding-aware routing, B request validity disproven+guarded, C breaker disposition, D canned-gate preservation) are complete with tests. Defect A also ships the funding RECOVERY USER JOURNEY: the LLM service emits credit-exhaustion transitions via an injected observer (fires only on genuine transitions, silent on startup-restore), and the daemon turns them into one deduped operator notification each — on pause it names the provider + both recovery paths (top up -> auto-retry ~1h, or `circuit reset` now), on recovery it confirms back-in-rotation (topic=breakers, always-on operational health, not agency-policy gated). ProviderStatus now carries credit_tripped/credit_probing so the dashboard/API/`circuit status` surface funding state. New persistence: runtime_settings.provider_funding_state; new observability edge: provider funding transition -> operator notification. UPDATE 2026-06-16 (D residual closed): audited every ErrGuardExhausted emitter — the three structurally-identical fail-closed terminal branches (standard guard exhaustion, verifier retry, verifier evidence recovery) all emit the generic 'couldn't get a confident answer' line precisely when blockedOutcomeForGuardExhaustion returns ('',false) for a fail-closed violation. RCA from recorded state (the retained behavior-soak state.db): the honest-blocker path WAS firing for the violations exercised (3x execution_truth/artifact -> file-write blocker; web/introspection/internal_protocol covered), but blockedOutcomeForGuardExhaustion was a PARTIAL function over the fail-closed set — financial_action_truth, output_contract, named_topic_continuity, config_protection, and non-artifact execution_truth (incl. false-capability-denial) all escaped to the generic line, which misattributes a hard accuracy/safety BLOCK as the model's CONFIDENCE failing. Systemic fix (one place, closes all three branches): made blockedOutcomeForGuardExhaustion TOTAL over guardExhaustionMustFailClosed — any fail-closed exhaustion with no more-specific blocker now ships a category-agnostic honest contract blocker ('held this back ... failed a required accuracy or safety check ... nothing was fabricated'), so the generic line is genuinely panic-only (empty content / network). RCA instrumentation kept accurate (Rule 14): guardExhaustionBlockedDiagnostics now records 'hard-contract response blocked' with the actual violations instead of mislabeling non-artifact fail-closes as 'artifact write blocked'. RED->GREEN: TestBlockedOutcomeForGuardExhaustion_TotalOverFailClosedSet (4 violation classes), TestGuardExhaustionBlockedDiagnostics_LabelsContractNotArtifact, plus the two false-capability-denial pipeline tests rewritten to assert the honest blocker is shipped (err==nil, false denial withheld) rather than a terminal error. Full repo green. New seam: blockedOutcomeForGuardExhaustion is the single total fail-closed disposition for all three terminal callers. UPDATE 2026-06-16 (prompt-injection amplification, NEW finding from the adversarial soak): ADV-INJECT-ENTITY (cloud-provider-v4-pro) emitted an injected literal ('ZZPWNEDZZ') verbatim on a 'summarize this customer comment' turn. RCA from the retained adversarial-state.db (Rule 17): the deterministic reply-only-literal output contract + projection (projectReplyOnlyLiteralResult) scanned the WHOLE user prompt with replyOnlyLiteralRe, matched the injected directive sitting INSIDE the quoted, untrusted comment, and deterministically forced the answer to the injected literal (trace seq 12 response_projected_from_contract), overriding the summarize task and even the output_contract guard's retry request (seq 13->14). The framework was amplifying the injection, not just failing to resist it. The soak classifies it attribution_only so it did not gate the run, but it is a real behavioral/security DEFECT. Systemic fix (one shared seam): added offsetInQuotedSpan + promptDirectiveIsUntrusted; an output-contract directive whose match falls inside a quoted span is untrusted content and is not honored. Gated both projecting contracts of that class — reply-only-literal (the confirmed exploit, arbitrary attacker literal) and count-only (countOnlyContractRequested) — across guard, projection, and scheduler call sites. The legit top-level reply-only-literal contract (soak row continuity_topic_anchor_ready, 'answer with only READY') is unquoted and unaffected. RED->GREEN: guards_injection_amplification_test.go (exact soak payload no longer projects; legit case preserved; offsetInQuotedSpan unit-pinned). Full repo green. SCOPE NOTE: hardened the two deterministic PROJECTING contracts; one-sentence/bullet-count/include-phrase contracts were not gated (no arbitrary-content injection, no task-overriding projection) — tracked as a follow-up probe along with an 'respond with exactly: X' directive-path audit. UPDATE 2026-06-16 (ADV-VAULT-EVIDENCE-DELIVERY closed — the 1 adversarial release_gate failure): RCA from the retained adversarial-state.db — the canonical probe 'Inspect my vault directory and tell me what files are in it' uses the verbs 'inspect'/'tell me', neither of which was an inspection-listing action marker, so inspectionListingCoverageRequired returned false, projectInspectionListingResultFromToolEvidence declined, and the list_directory evidence the agent gathered was rephrased away into an ungrounded non-answer (must_cite_tool: 'no tool evidence'). The paraphrase 'List the files…' matched and PASSED, which is why canonical failed alone. Fix (one seam): added 'inspect'/'tell me' to the inspection-listing action markers (guards_truthfulness_inspection.go), so the delivery projection recognizes the intent and DELIVERS the observed entries instead of restating the question (coverage-0 is not satisfiable by rephrasing). The predicate's guard callers were already satisfied by the tool-results condition, so this changes delivery, not rejection. Unit RED→GREEN (TestProjectInspectionListing_DeliversVaultListing_ADVVaultEvidenceDelivery); full repo green. The live adversarial re-run (clone-mode) is the pre-promote confirmation. Net: both adversarial non-passes (this + ADV-INJECT-ENTITY) are now fixed; the behavioral soak gate is otherwise 38/38 all-A on the bundled embedder.

required.title: Cron POST idempotency
required.summary: CreateJobRecord is idempotent: a re-POST of the same job (name+schedule+agent natural key) returns the existing job via db.CronRepository.FindEquivalentJob instead of creating a duplicate. +test.

required.title: Decompose internal/daemon god file + god function (daemon.go/daemon_subsystems.go files + New() constructor)
required.summary: DELIVERED (roadmap split-oversized-daemon-files + the daemon.New god-function decomposition, folded in 2026-06-15). FILES: daemon.go (1411L) + daemon_subsystems.go (932L) decomposed into 11 same-package siblings, all under the ~500-line cap: daemon.go (struct+Router+mergeLoadedSkills), daemon_init.go (daemonBuilder + New orchestrator), daemon_build.go (channels/pipeline/support phases), daemon_phases.go (5 boot-phase builders), daemon_service.go, daemon_lifecycle.go, daemon_tools.go, daemon_subsystems.go (run+cron+listener), daemon_heartbeats.go, daemon_pollers.go, daemon_agency.go. Subsystems + the service/tools/lifecycle portions were pure verbatim relocation. FUNCTION: the 728L New() god constructor was decomposed (behavior-preserving) to a 50-line ordered orchestrator over a daemonBuilder struct that accumulates the in-progress subsystems as fields; each of the 12 boot phases is a small phase* method (reusing the 5 buildX free helpers). The builder capture cleanly resolves the appState->daemonRef and appState-self forward references (closures read b.daemonRef/b.appState at call time). bgWorker/errBus/ks and the ready-guard cleanup defer stay in New so error-cleanup semantics are preserved; webhook fields are interface-typed so an absent adapter stays a nil interface (no typed-nil trap). Verified: gofmt/build/vet, the unit-test suite ./internal/daemon/ (incl. the New() constructor tests) and the unit-test suite -race all green.
evidence: internal/daemon/daemon_init.go (daemonBuilder struct + 50-line New orchestrator + thin phase methods)

required.title: Agency/autonomy slider stop-dot alignment fix
required.summary: Operator-reported: the discrete agency/autonomy slider stops looked misaligned. Root cause: dots sat in flex columns centered at (i+0.5)/N while the native thumb lands at i/(N-1). Fix: pin the thumb geometry (16px) and INSET the slider by half a column minus the thumb radius (calc((0.5/N)*100% - 8px)) so the thumb lands at exactly (i+0.5)/N — the column center — putting the thumb, its dot, and its label in one vertical line (labels stay pinned under their dots, operator requirement). Verified with a standalone render.

required.title: Repo-wide god-file/function decomposition (source + tests)
required.summary: DELIVERED + audited. Full repo-wide sweep: original 73 source files >500L + 51 funcs >120L + 56 test files >500L decomposed by responsibility (pure relocation for files, behavior-preserving helper extraction for funcs), verified package-by-package and by full suite. Post-split audit (whole-tree re-scan, source+test): ZERO source god functions, ZERO source god files except schema_ddl.go (indivisible DDL raw-string data literal); ZERO test files >500; 9 remaining test funcs >120 are all table-driven case lists (documented data-literal-equivalent exceptions). Full build/vet/gofmt/test green throughout.

required.title: Upgrade prompt: offer the bundled embedder in layman language
required.summary: On upgrade, detect users still on the legacy (Embedded Runtime) embedder and prompt them to enable the bundled local embedder in NON-TECHNICAL language (no 'sidecar/embedder/vector'). New installs default to it. Opt-in: Enable / Keep current / Ask later. SHIPPED 2026-06-16: daemon.maybePromptEmbedderUpgrade fires once at startup (wired in run() after wireFundingNotifications) when EffectiveEmbeddingEngine != bundled. Jargon-free operator copy (embedderUpgradeOperatorText, test-pinned to exclude sidecar/vector/gguf/llama/embed-model) surfaced via the established async-notice convention (operator startup log + a config.embedder_upgrade dashboard event on TopicConfig). Persisted once-per-version in runtime_settings (embedder_upgrade_prompt_version) so the three options are honored without nagging: Enable = run the one printed `flowforge config set memory.embedding_engine bundled` command; Keep current = do nothing; Ask later = silent until the next version bump re-surfaces it. Pure shouldPromptEmbedderUpgrade decision unit-tested (bundled never prompts; legacy prompts once per version; re-prompts after upgrade). New installs resolve to bundled and never see it.

required.title: Agent Task Tracking Board (kanban / list / timeline)
required.summary: Surfaces the durable tasks table plus new conversational work-output tasks as a TOP-LEVEL Tasks dashboard page in three views (kanban lanes x status, list, timeline). Built 2026-06-16 per docs/plans/2026-06-09-agent-task-tracking-board.md: migration 070 adds tasks.origin_session_id; a post-turn pipeline capture hook records a work_output task when the turn executed a work-product OperationClass (artifact write / state change / outbound action), deduped per origin session, off the hot path; a 'tasks' WS topic + grouped snapshot feed the UI; client-side source + completed-age filters. Unit-tested: repo grouping/filters/dedup + the capture gate (the 5 spec acceptance tests). VALIDATED: the post-turn capture surface was exercised live in the bundled-embedder behavioral soak (7 work_output tasks captured from real tool-executing turns, no regression) and re-confirmed in the 2026-06-17 release-gate re-run (38/38 on 45b16afd). Release sequencing RESOLVED: ships in v1.2.0 (folded into the v1.2.0 CHANGELOG).
evidence: src/engine/task_board_capture.go (work-product gate + capture hook, with tests)

deferred.title: Voice phone channel (open-source / low-cost stack)
deferred.summary: Large new external channel: let users reach the agent over a phone call. Operator-selected for its own release with a dedicated soak rather than bundling into v1.2.0. REVISED 2026-06-16: build on an OPEN-SOURCE, low-or-no-cost stack (operator preference) rather than the originally-floated paid managed vendor (Voice Vendor RealtimeAgents). GATED on realtime video/audio ingestion landing first (roadmap item realtime-av-ingestion): a phone call is one consumer of the general realtime A/V ingestion substrate, so that capability ships before the phone channel.
deferred.rationale: Scope/sequencing: a new external channel warrants its own release + soak; bundling it would balloon the v1.2.0 test surface. Now also sequenced AFTER realtime-av-ingestion (operator decision 2026-06-16) and re-scoped to an open-source / low-no-cost stack instead of a paid vendor. Deferral authority: operator, 2026-06-15 (re-scoped 2026-06-16).
